1	# Peer Review Incorporation Round 1
2	
3	## Decision
4	
5	The user selected full incorporation of all round 1 blockers and nits.
6	
7	## Incorporated Blockers
8	
9	- `B-001`: Added explicit extensibility, agent-manageability, extension Host API, hooks, native tools, MCP/tooling, bridge SDK, bundles/registries, skills/capabilities, and config lifecycle analysis.
10	- `B-002`: Added concrete SQLite migration version, final DDL, migration transaction expectations, rebuild/copy/drop behavior, delete targets, and migration tests.
11	- `B-003`: Added concrete Go definitions for wire models, store summaries, query DTOs, `ConversationStore`, `Work`, `WorkState`, validators, reason codes, and old-to-new symbol renames.
12	- `B-004`: Added deterministic direct-room resolution algorithm, direct ID hashing shape, uniqueness constraints, and concurrent resolve semantics.
13	- `B-005`: Added durable `network_work` table and authoritative cross-container work binding rules.
14	- `B-006`: Added explicit `work_id` / `task_runs` relationship: `work_id` is network-level lifecycle metadata, while `task_runs` remains the only durable queue.
15	- `B-007`: Added same-transaction `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` write strategy for timeline rows, work binding, participant updates, summaries, and audit rows.
16	- `B-008`: Added RFC 004 trust integration and signed-field set for `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, and `work_id`.
17	
18	## Incorporated Nits
19	
20	- `N-001`: Added explicit TanStack Router file/route map for `/network` threads and direct rooms.
21	- `N-002`: Added CLI commands, flags, deleted flags, and JSON/JSONL output shapes.
22	- `N-003`: Stated `ConversationStore` is consumed in `internal/network`, implemented in `internal/store/globaldb`, and no `internal/conversation` package is introduced.
23	- `N-004`: Added explicit old-to-new Go symbol rename table.
24	- `N-005`: Aligned MVP task 01-08 with implementation build order.
25	- `N-006`: Stated archived `.compozy/tasks/_archived/*` artifacts may retain old terminology.
26	- `N-007`: Stated expanding `direct_room` beyond two peers is a future schema migration trigger.
27	- `N-008`: Added metric names, units, labels, and cardinality rules.
28	- `N-009`: Added 80% touched-package coverage floor and Linux-Race parity expectations.
29	- `N-010`: Added bundled `agh-network` skill rewrite outline and example envelopes.
30	
31	## Deferred Items
32	
33	None.
34	
35	## Files Changed
36	
37	- `.compozy/tasks/network-threads/_techspec.md`
38	- `.compozy/tasks/network-threads/adrs/adr-001.md`
39	- `.compozy/tasks/network-threads/adrs/adr-002.md`
40	- `.compozy/tasks/network-threads/adrs/adr-003.md`
41	- `.codex/ledger/2026-05-04-MEMORY-network-threads.md`
42	- `.compozy/tasks/network-threads/qa/peer-review-incorporation-round1.md`
43	
44	## Research Inputs Applied
45	
46	- Current `globalSchemaMigrations` ends at version 15, so the spec names version 16 unless another migration lands first.
47	- Current network persistence uses flat `network_timeline_log` rows with `interaction_id`, so the spec replaces the write path with a same-transaction conversation write.
48	- Existing store patterns use rebuild migrations for SQLite table shape changes and `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` helpers for multi-step write transactions.
49	- Current HTTP/UDS/CLI surfaces expose flat channel and peer message paths, so the spec deletes those as primary conversation paths and replaces them with thread/direct endpoints.
50	- Current native tools expose network status/channels/inbox/peers/send, so the spec updates `network_send` and adds conversation-specific tools.
51	- Current hook taxonomy has no `network` family, so the spec adds async observation-only network hook events.
52	- RFC 004 signs the full envelope excluding only `proof.sig`, so the spec makes the new conversation fields explicit in the signed content.

1	# ADR-001: Separate Public Threads from Direct Rooms
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Date
8	
9	2026-05-04
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	AGH Network v0 currently mixes public channel discussion and bilateral handoff semantics across a flat channel timeline plus a peer-room projection. The operator wants Slack-like public thread navigation for N-to-N channel discussion, while preserving restricted one-to-one agent conversations as a distinct product surface. Treating both as one universal container forces visibility, addressing, and workflow semantics into the same primitive and makes the UI ambiguous.
14	
15	## Decision
16	
17	AGH Network will model two channel-scoped conversation containers:
18	
19	- `public_thread` for public N-to-N conversation inside a channel
20	- `direct_room` for restricted one-to-one conversation inside a channel
21	
22	`channel` remains the audience/discovery scope above both containers. `peer` remains an identity participant, not a conversation container. Peer activity views remain auxiliary UX filters and are not the same thing as `direct_room`.
23	
24	`direct_room` is intentionally two-party only in this cut. The runtime derives a stable `direct_id` from `(channel, sorted(peer_a, peer_b))` using a domain-separated SHA-256 hash and persists `(channel, peer_a, peer_b)` under a unique constraint. The resolver must be order-independent and concurrency-safe.
25	
26	## Alternatives Considered
27	
28	### Alternative 1: Universal thread container for all conversation
29	
30	- **Description**: Every message, including bilateral handoffs, lives in one thread model. Peer rooms are derived views only.
31	- **Pros**: Fewer protocol primitives; one conversation model.
32	- **Cons**: Conflates public discussion and restricted conversation; makes visibility rules hard to explain; forces bilateral handoff noise into public-thread semantics.
33	- **Why rejected**: The product goal explicitly wants public thread UX and restricted bilateral direct UX to stay distinct.
34	
35	### Alternative 2: Keep flat channel timelines plus peer rooms only
36	
37	- **Description**: Preserve current channel + peer split and add more grouping metadata without first-class public threads.
38	- **Pros**: Smaller change set; closer to current runtime.
39	- **Cons**: Does not solve parallel public-topic mixing in channels; keeps the main UX problem intact.
40	- **Why rejected**: It patches symptoms instead of creating a clean public conversation model.
41	
42	## Consequences
43	
44	### Positive
45	
46	- Public discussion and bilateral handoff become separate, legible product concepts.
47	- Visibility rules become explicit instead of inferred from message kind.
48	- Web navigation can become `channel -> public threads` and `channel -> direct rooms`.
49	
50	### Negative
51	
52	- The protocol and store gain one more conversation primitive.
53	- Existing `/network` channel and peer timeline assumptions become obsolete.
54	
55	### Risks
56	
57	- Direct-room identity could become underspecified if pair resolution is vague.
58	- Overlapping thread/direct affordances could still confuse users if naming is sloppy.
59	- Expanding `direct_room` beyond two peers would invalidate the current `peer_a` / `peer_b` schema and must be treated as a future migration with a new ADR.
60	
61	## Implementation Notes
62	
63	- `public_thread` and `direct_room` are both channel-scoped.
64	- No private-thread feature lands in this cut.
65	- No group direct-room feature lands in this cut.
66	- `network_direct_rooms` stores `peer_a` and `peer_b` in lexicographic order with `UNIQUE(channel, peer_a, peer_b)` and `CHECK(peer_a < peer_b)`.
67	- Resolve-only operations must not update `last_activity_at`; only message writes move activity.
68	- The implementation must delete the current mental model that “peer room is the primary direct conversation primitive” without replacing it with another ambiguous projection.
69	
70	## References
71	
72	- `docs/rfcs/003_agh-network-v0.md`
73	- `web/src/hooks/routes/use-network-page.ts`
74	- `web/src/systems/network/components/network-workspace-shell.tsx`
75	- `docs/ideas/complex-scenarios/network.md`
76	
```

1	# ADR-002: Rename interaction_id to work_id and narrow it to lifecycle-bearing work
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Date
8	
9	2026-05-04
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	`interaction_id` in AGH Network v0 currently carries both “work or conversation progression” language and lifecycle semantics. With first-class public threads and direct rooms, the name becomes actively misleading: conversation grouping belongs to the conversation container, while lifecycle state belongs to directed work. Greenfield alpha allows a hard cut now, before external adoption makes ambiguity expensive.
14	
15	## Decision
16	
17	Perform a hard cut rename from `interaction_id` to `work_id` across RFCs, runtime, store, API contracts, CLI, web, docs, prompts, and tests. `work_id` names a lifecycle-bearing unit of directed work inside exactly one conversation container.
18	
19	`work_id` is not a conversation identifier. A conversation may contain zero, one, or many work units. A work unit never spans multiple conversation containers.
20	
21	`work_id` is a network-level lifecycle marker in this cut, not a task queue. `task_runs` remains the only durable queue and the only owner of claim-token, lease, heartbeat, complete, fail, release, and cancel transitions.
22	
23	## Alternatives Considered
24	
25	### Alternative 1: Keep interaction_id and redefine the docs only
26	
27	- **Description**: Preserve the field name but explain it as work-only from now on.
28	- **Pros**: Less implementation churn in the short term.
29	- **Cons**: Keeps a misleading name in the protocol forever; leaks ambiguity into prompts, docs, and code review.
30	- **Why rejected**: Greenfield hard-cut timing makes this the cheapest moment to remove the ambiguity.
31	
32	### Alternative 2: Collapse work and conversation into thread_id
33	
34	- **Description**: Remove `interaction_id` and let thread identity carry lifecycle.
35	- **Pros**: Fewer identifiers.
36	- **Cons**: Threads should not complete/fail/cancel; one thread can contain multiple concurrent work units.
37	- **Why rejected**: Conflates conversation topology with work lifecycle semantics.
38	
39	## Consequences
40	
41	### Positive
42	
43	- `thread_id` / `direct_id` mean conversation; `work_id` means work.
44	- Agent prompts and operator docs become materially easier to understand.
45	- Lifecycle logic stays intact without owning conversation grouping.
46	
47	### Negative
48	
49	- This is a breaking rename across every exported surface.
50	- Existing tests, OpenAPI, and web generated types all need to move in the same change.
51	
52	### Risks
53	
54	- Partial rename would leave the repo in an inconsistent state.
55	- Archived docs and examples may keep stale terminology unless explicitly updated or marked historical.
56	
57	## Implementation Notes
58	
59	- Delete targets include all `interaction_id` fields, docs text, validation paths, query params, and examples on supported current surfaces.
60	- Archived artifacts can remain historical, but active docs and runtime surfaces must hard cut.
61	- Store `work_id` in `network_work`, bound to exactly one `(channel, surface, thread_id|direct_id)` container.
62	- Network code must correlate task ingress through `trace_id`, `causation_id`, and task metadata; it must not reuse `work_id` as `task_runs.id`.
63	- Required hard-cut symbol renames:
64	  - `Envelope.InteractionID` -> `Envelope.WorkID`
65	  - `NetworkSendRequest.InteractionID` -> `NetworkSendRequest.WorkID`
66	  - `NetworkSendPayload.InteractionID` -> `NetworkSendPayload.WorkID`
67	  - `NetworkMessageEntry.InteractionID` -> `NetworkConversationMessage.WorkID`
68	  - `InteractionState` -> `WorkState`
69	  - `Interaction` -> `Work`
70	  - `OpenInteraction` -> `OpenWork`
71	  - `ApplyInteractionEnvelope` -> `ApplyWorkEnvelope`
72	  - `ReasonCodeInteractionClosed` -> `ReasonCodeWorkClosed`
73	  - CLI `--interaction-id` -> `--work`
74	- No alias or compatibility reader may accept `interaction_id` on active surfaces.
75	
76	## References
77	
78	- `docs/rfcs/003_agh-network-v0.md`
79	- `internal/network/lifecycle.go`
80	- `internal/api/contract/contract.go`
81	- `packages/site/content/protocol/interactions.mdx`
82	
```

1	# ADR-003: Make direct a conversation surface, not a message kind
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Date
8	
9	2026-05-04
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	In AGH Network v0, `kind:"direct"` mixes three dimensions:
14	
15	- end-user visibility expectations
16	- addressing semantics
17	- event type
18	
19	Once `public_thread` and `direct_room` are both explicit conversation containers, `direct` as a message kind becomes redundant and confusing. Message kinds should describe what happened; conversation surface should describe where it happened.
20	
21	## Decision
22	
23	Introduce an explicit conversation surface on conversation-bearing envelopes:
24	
25	- `surface:"thread"`
26	- `surface:"direct"`
27	
28	Delete `kind:"direct"` from the protocol. Keep event kinds focused on event semantics:
29	
30	- `greet`
31	- `whois`
32	- `say`
33	- `capability`
34	- `receipt`
35	- `trace`
36	
37	Conversation-bearing messages require either:
38	
39	- `surface:"thread"` with `thread_id`
40	- `surface:"direct"` with `direct_id`
41	
42	RFC 004 signed content must include `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, and `work_id` when those fields are present. Trust validation happens before routing, and routing uses `channel + surface + container id + to`.
43	
44	## Alternatives Considered
45	
46	### Alternative 1: Keep kind=direct and add surface temporarily
47	
48	- **Description**: Add surface but allow `kind:"direct"` for compatibility or reduced churn.
49	- **Pros**: Smaller implementation delta.
50	- **Cons**: Preserves the core ambiguity and teaches two overlapping ways to describe the same thing.
51	- **Why rejected**: The user explicitly chose the full hard cut while the project is still pre-launch.
52	
53	### Alternative 2: Keep visibility encoded only in `to`
54	
55	- **Description**: Let `to` imply bilateral restricted conversation without a surface field.
56	- **Pros**: Minimal wire change.
57	- **Cons**: `to` is addressing, not visibility or container identity; does not model a direct room cleanly.
58	- **Why rejected**: Endereçamento and visibilidade should not share one overloaded primitive.
59	
60	## Consequences
61	
62	### Positive
63	
64	- Protocol vocabulary becomes orthogonal: `kind` = event, `surface` = conversation container.
65	- Public threads can carry targeted work visibly without pretending to be private.
66	- Direct rooms become explicit protocol/runtime surfaces instead of UI-only projections.
67	
68	### Negative
69	
70	- All message validation, examples, CLI help, and docs must change together.
71	- Existing `direct`-specific lifecycle language has to be rewritten.
72	
73	### Risks
74	
75	- If `surface` rules are not enforced consistently, visibility bugs could appear in API/Web layers.
76	- If direct-room IDs are unstable, bilateral history can fragment.
77	
78	## Implementation Notes
79	
80	- `greet` and `whois` remain non-conversation kinds and MUST NOT carry `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, or `work_id`.
81	- `say`, `capability`, `receipt`, and `trace` become conversation-bearing kinds.
82	- Normal direct-room chat is `kind:"say"` with `surface:"direct"`; `DirectBody` is deleted.
83	- `receipt` and `trace` inherit visibility from the conversation container where their `work_id` is bound.
84	- In AGH Runtime, control-plane ergonomics may auto-resolve `thread_id` or `direct_id`, but the wire contract stays explicit.
85	- `kind:"direct"` must be rejected by envelope validation, HTTP, UDS, CLI, native tools, extension Host API, generated contracts, and docs examples.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	- `docs/rfcs/003_agh-network-v0.md`
90	- `internal/network/envelope.go`
91	- `internal/network/validate.go`
92	- `internal/skills/bundled/skills/agh-network/SKILL.md`
93	
```

1	# TechSpec: AGH Network Conversation Containers and Work Threads
2	
3	## Executive Summary
4	
5	This TechSpec redesigns AGH Network v0 around explicit conversation containers instead of a flat channel timeline plus peer-room projection. The accepted model keeps `channel` as the audience and discovery scope, introduces `public_thread` as the public N-to-N conversation primitive, introduces `direct_room` as the restricted one-to-one conversation primitive, and renames `interaction_id` to `work_id` for lifecycle-bearing work inside either conversation type.
6	
7	No PRD exists for this workstream; this TechSpec is authored directly from the user-approved product direction, codebase research, the local agent-network knowledge base, and the round 1 Claude/Opus peer review. The primary trade-off is a larger hard cut now in exchange for a cleaner protocol, sharper UX semantics, deterministic storage invariants, and less permanent ambiguity in every public surface.
8	
9	This is a greenfield alpha hard cut. The implementation must not ship compatibility aliases, dual fields, shadow migrations, or fallback readers for the old flat conversation model.
10	
11	## MVP Boundary
12	
13	MVP boundary for the upcoming task decomposition: tasks 01-08 must implement the complete hard cut for AGH Network conversation containers.
14	
15	1. Task 01: Rewrite RFCs, glossary, and protocol examples for `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, and `work_id`.
16	2. Task 02: Update shared runtime envelope types, validators, direct-room ID derivation, work lifecycle enums, reason codes, and hard-cut Go symbols.
17	3. Task 03: Ship the numbered SQLite migration, final fresh-db schema, conversation side tables, `network_work`, same-transaction writes, store queries, and migration tests.
18	4. Task 04: Update router, delivery wrappers, lifecycle handling, hooks, observability, startup prompts, and bundled agent skills.
19	5. Task 05: Update HTTP, UDS, CLI, native tools, extension Host API, OpenAPI, and generated TypeScript in one change.
20	6. Task 06: Rebuild the `/network` web experience around channel threads and direct rooms.
21	7. Task 07: Rewrite site/runtime/operator docs, extension SDK docs, examples, and MCP/skill-facing guidance.
22	8. Task 08: Add unit, integration, web, E2E, and real-scenario QA coverage, then run required verification gates.
23	
24	Post-MVP work is explicitly deferred: private group threads, more than two participants in restricted rooms, thread split/merge, cross-channel thread moves, unread-count sync, notification preferences, search ranking, retention policy controls, transcript export, federation policy, cryptographic private threads, and analytics dashboards beyond the metrics listed here.
25	
26	Out of scope: compatibility aliases for `interaction_id`, accepting `kind:"direct"` after this change, migration bridges for old alpha conversation rows, public-thread embedded direct messages, work items that span multiple conversation containers, direct rooms with more than two peers, and any UI that renders non-implemented runtime controls.
27	
28	Historical exception: archived `.compozy/tasks/_archived/*` artifacts may retain old `interaction_id` and `kind:"direct"` terminology because they are provenance records, not active product documentation. Active RFCs, docs, generated contracts, prompts, tests, and examples must hard cut.
29	
30	## System Architecture
31	
32	### Component Overview
33	
34	| Component | Purpose | Boundary |
35	| --- | --- | --- |
36	| RFC / envelope model | Replace `kind:"direct"` with `surface:"thread"|"direct"` plus `thread_id` / `direct_id` and `work_id` | Protocol + shared runtime types |
37	| Network router | Route by channel and conversation container, preserve work lifecycle inside the container | `internal/network` only |
38	| Conversation registry | Materialize thread heads, direct rooms, participants, and active work for query/UI use | `internal/store/globaldb` |
39	| Message store | Persist `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, `work_id`, and visibility-scoped timeline rows | `internal/store` + `internal/store/globaldb` |
40	| Task domain | Remain the only durable task queue when network messages create task runs | `internal/task` only |
41	| CLI / HTTP / UDS | Expose thread/direct listing, detail, message, resolve, and send surfaces symmetrically | control plane |
42	| Native tools / MCP | Give agents a machine-readable path to inspect and speak in containers | `internal/tools`, `internal/daemon` |
43	| Extension Host API | Give extensions parity with HTTP/UDS for read/send/resolve operations | `internal/extension` |
44	| Web workspace | Replace flat channel timeline + peer-first model with channel threads and direct rooms | `web/` |
45	| Bundled prompts / skills | Teach agents to respond in the current conversation container and use `work_id` only for lifecycle-bearing work | startup prompt + bundled skills |
46	| Docs / OpenAPI | Rewrite protocol/runtime docs and regenerate codegen in the same PR | `packages/site`, `openapi`, `web/src/generated` |
47	
48	### Data Flow
49	
50	1. A local session sends a conversation-bearing message through CLI, HTTP, UDS, native tool, extension Host API, or bridge ingress.
51	2. The daemon validates `kind`, `surface`, conversation ID, `work_id`, `reply_to`, trust proof, and kind-specific body rules.
52	3. The router resolves the target container:
53	   - `surface:"thread"` -> `(channel, thread_id)`
54	   - `surface:"direct"` -> `(channel, direct_id)`
55	4. For direct rooms, control-plane helpers may resolve or create the room before send; the wire envelope still carries the explicit `direct_id`.
56	5. The store persists the normalized message row, binds or advances `work_id` when present, updates participants, and updates conversation summaries in the same SQLite transaction.
57	6. Delivery logic enqueues inbound prompts for local sessions and renders wrappers with conversation metadata.
58	7. HTTP/UDS/CLI/Web query persisted conversation views instead of reconstructing them from flat channel data.
59	
60	## Architectural Boundaries
61	
62	- `internal/network` owns protocol envelope validation, routing decisions, direct-room ID derivation, work lifecycle state transitions, and delivery wrapper metadata. It must not import `internal/api`, `internal/cli`, `web`, `packages/site`, or generated OpenAPI code.
63	- `internal/store` owns typed persistence DTOs and validation helpers for durable network records. It can be imported by `internal/network`.
64	- `internal/store/globaldb` owns SQLite schema, migrations, indexes, transaction helpers, and query implementations. It must not import `internal/network`; it implements interfaces consumed by `internal/network` through `internal/store` types.
65	- Do not create an `internal/conversation` package in this cut. `ConversationStore` is consumed in `internal/network` and implemented by `internal/store/globaldb`.
66	- `internal/task` remains the only package that owns durable task queue state and `task_runs` ownership transitions. Network code may observe or correlate task activity but must not duplicate `ClaimNextRun`, leases, or terminal state transitions.
67	- `internal/api/contract` owns DTO shape and OpenAPI generation inputs. Contract changes must co-ship with `openapi/agh.json` and `web/src/generated/agh-openapi.d.ts`.
68	- `internal/api/core` owns HTTP handler validation and response assembly. It may call runtime/store services, but it must not bypass `internal/network` validation for send semantics.
69	- `internal/cli` and UDS clients are control-plane adapters. They must call the same contract/runtime primitives as HTTP and must not implement separate conversation resolution rules.
70	- `web/` owns operator UX state, routing, and rendering only. It must treat conversation summaries and messages as server truth and must not infer direct-room membership from flat channel rows.
71	- `packages/site` owns documentation prose and examples only. It must reflect the RFC and generated contract; it must not define independent protocol fields.
72	- `cmd/agh` and `internal/daemon` remain the composition root. New conversation services are constructed there or under the existing daemon composition boundary.
73	
74	## Terminology
75	
76	| Term | Definition |
77	| --- | --- |
78	| `channel` | Audience, discovery, and permission scope for public threads and direct rooms. |
79	| `public_thread` | Public N-to-N conversation container inside one channel. Wire value: `surface:"thread"`. |
80	| `direct_room` | Restricted one-to-one conversation container inside one channel. Wire value: `surface:"direct"`. |
81	| `message` | One AGH Network envelope/event persisted in a conversation or discovery stream. |
82	| `work_id` | Lifecycle-bearing work marker bound to exactly one conversation container. |
83	| `reply_to` | Fine-grained message-to-message edge. It never substitutes for `thread_id` or `direct_id`. |
84	| `trace_id` | Operational correlation across messages, handoffs, and task ingress. It never substitutes for `work_id`. |
85	| `causation_id` | Event/message that caused the current message. |
86	| `peer` | Participant identity. A peer is not a conversation container. |

141	### Kind Rules

143	| Kind | Conversation fields | Work field | Addressing |

150	| `trace` | MUST carry `surface` and matching container ID | MUST carry `work_id` | `to` MAY target the work initiator |

425	### Direct Room Resolution Algorithm
426	
427	Direct room resolution is deterministic and race-safe. It does not depend on message order.
428	
429	Inputs:
430	
431	```go
432	type DirectRoomResolveRequest struct {
433		SessionID string `json:"session_id"`
434		PeerID    string `json:"peer_id"`
435	}
436	```
437	
438	Algorithm in `internal/network`:
439	
440	```go
441	func DirectRoomIdentity(channel string, localPeer string, remotePeer string) (directID string, peerA string, peerB string, err error) {
442		// 1. Trim and validate channel with the existing channel grammar.
443		// 2. Trim and validate both peer IDs with ValidatePeerID.
444		// 3. Reject identical peers.
445		// 4. Sort peers lexicographically into peerA, peerB.
446		// 5. Hash domain-separated identity bytes:
447		//    "agh-network/direct-room/v1\x00" + channel + "\x00" + peerA + "\x00" + peerB
448		// 6. Return "direct_" + first 32 lowercase hex chars of SHA-256.
449	}
450	```
451	
452	Store semantics in `internal/store/globaldb`:
453	
454	1. Open a network immediate transaction using the same pattern as `withTaskImmediateTransaction`.
455	2. `INSERT OR IGNORE` `(channel, direct_id, peer_a, peer_b, opened_at, last_activity_at, message_count, open_work_count)` into `network_direct_rooms`.
456	3. `SELECT` the row by `(channel, peer_a, peer_b)` inside the same transaction.
457	4. If the selected `direct_id` does not equal the deterministic ID, return `ErrDirectRoomCollision`.
458	5. Do not update `last_activity_at` on resolve-only calls; only message writes move activity.
459	
460	DDL enforces:
461	
462	- `PRIMARY KEY (channel, direct_id)`
463	- `UNIQUE (channel, peer_a, peer_b)`
464	- `CHECK (peer_a < peer_b)`
465	
466	Concurrent calls for the same `(channel, peer_a, peer_b)` return the same row. Concurrent calls for different pairs never share a row. Expanding `direct_room` beyond two peers is a future schema migration trigger because `peer_a` / `peer_b` and the unique pair constraint are intentionally two-party only.
467	
468	### Work Lifecycle and `task_runs`
469	
470	`work_id` is a network-level lifecycle marker in v0. It is not a task queue, not a claim token, not a task-run ID, and not a replacement for `task_runs`.
471	
472	Rules:
473	
474	- `network_work` binds `work_id` to exactly one conversation container and tracks only network lifecycle state.
475	- `task_runs` remains the single durable work queue and the only owner of task claim, lease, heartbeat, complete, fail, release, and cancel transitions.
476	- A network message may cause task ingress through existing task-domain APIs. That task run is correlated by `trace_id`, `causation_id`, and task metadata, not by reusing `work_id` as queue ownership.
477	- Network code must never call `ClaimNextRun`, mutate task-run claim tokens, or set task-run terminal state directly.
478	- If a work conversation needs an executable task, the implementation calls the existing task service ingress path and records the resulting task/run correlation in task metadata or observability summaries, not in `network_work` as a queue.
479	- Raw `claim_token` remains forbidden in network envelopes, message bodies, metadata, logs, prompts, HTTP/UDS responses, CLI output, and web UI.
480	
481	### Same-Transaction Write Strategy
482	
483	`WriteConversationMessage` replaces `WriteNetworkMessage` as the authoritative write path.
484	
485	Required transaction shape:
486	
487	```go
488	func (g *GlobalDB) withNetworkImmediateTransaction(
489		ctx context.Context,
490		action string,
491		run func(exec networkSQLExecutor) error,
492	) (err error) {
493		conn, err := g.db.Conn(ctx)
494		if err != nil {
495			return fmt.Errorf("store: open connection for %s: %w", action, err)
496		}
497		defer func() { _ = conn.Close() }()
498	
499		rollbackCtx := context.WithoutCancel(ctx)
500		if _, err := conn.ExecContext(ctx, "BEGIN IMMEDIATE"); err != nil {
501			return fmt.Errorf("store: begin immediate %s transaction: %w", action, err)
502		}
503	
504		finished := false
505		defer func() {
506			if !finished {
507				joinCleanupError(&err, rollbackImmediate(rollbackCtx, conn, action))
508			}
509		}()
510	
511		if err := run(conn); err != nil {
512			return err
513		}
514		if _, err = conn.ExecContext(ctx, "COMMIT"); err != nil {
515			return fmt.Errorf("store: commit %s transaction: %w", action, err)
516		}
517		finished = true
518		return nil
519	}
520	```
521	
522	Within one `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction:
523	
524	1. Validate the entry and normalize timestamps.
525	2. Insert `network_timeline_log` with `ON CONFLICT(message_id) DO NOTHING`.
526	3. If the insert was a duplicate, return `Duplicate=true` and do not update summaries.
527	4. Verify the target container exists or create it when this is a valid thread-opening or direct-room-opening write.
528	5. If `work_id` is present, select existing `network_work` row by `work_id`.
529	6. If no `network_work` row exists, insert one bound to the current container.
530	7. If a `network_work` row exists with a different `(channel, surface, thread_id, direct_id)`, reject with `ReasonCodeWorkContainerMismatch`.
531	8. If a `network_work` row is terminal and the new message is not an allowed terminal duplicate, reject with `ReasonCodeWorkClosed`.
532	9. Update `network_work.state`, `last_activity_at`, and `terminal_at` when the message advances lifecycle state.
533	10. Insert or update public-thread participants for `surface:"thread"`.
534	11. Update `network_threads` or `network_direct_rooms` summary counters, `last_activity_at`, `last_message_preview`, and `open_work_count`.
535	12. Write the matching `network_audit_log` row with the same container fields.
536	13. Commit.
537	
538	The implementation must not write timeline rows and summary rows in separate transactions. If summary update fails, the message insert rolls back.
539	
540	## Data Models
541	
542	### Field Rationale
543	
544	| Field | Shape | Purpose | Rationale |
545	| --- | --- | --- | --- |
546	| `surface` | nullable enum string: `thread` or `direct` | Selects the conversation container class for conversation-bearing messages | Nullable only for discovery kinds; separates where a message lives from what event happened |
547	| `thread_id` | nullable string | Identifies a public N-to-N conversation inside a channel | Nullable because direct-room messages use `direct_id`; indexed for thread timelines |
548	| `direct_id` | nullable string | Identifies a restricted one-to-one room inside a channel | Nullable because public-thread messages use `thread_id`; stable ID prevents fragmented bilateral history |
549	| `work_id` | nullable string | Identifies lifecycle-bearing work inside one conversation container | Replaces `interaction_id` and prevents lifecycle state from doubling as UX thread state |
550	| `reply_to` | nullable message ID | Points to the specific message being answered | Keeps fine-grained message causality separate from conversation membership |
551	| `trace_id` | nullable string | Correlates distributed runtime work and handoffs | Supports observability across related containers without reusing `work_id` |
552	| `causation_id` | nullable message ID or event ID | Records the event that caused this message | Supports auditability for summarize-back and handoff flows |
553	| `root_message_id` | non-null message ID on `network_threads` | Anchors the public thread head shown in the channel timeline | Avoids reconstructing thread heads from every message query |
554	| `participant_count` | integer on `network_threads` | Fast summary for web/channel thread lists | Matchable summary state belongs in a typed column, not JSON metadata |
555	| `peer_a`, `peer_b` | non-null peer IDs on `network_direct_rooms` | Defines direct-room membership | Enforces restricted visibility and stable one-to-one uniqueness |
556	| `open_work_count` | integer on thread/direct summary tables | Indicates active lifecycle work in a conversation | Supports operator triage without scanning all lifecycle events |
557	| `last_message_preview` | text on summary tables | Shows a safe preview in lists | Prevents web from querying full messages for list rendering |

770	### Side-Table vs JSON Decisions
771	
772	- `network_threads` is a side table because thread heads, last activity, message counts, open work counts, and participant counts are matchable state used by API queries and web navigation.
773	- `network_thread_participants` is a side table because participant counts must be exact, incrementally maintainable, and queryable by peer without scanning all messages.
774	- `network_direct_rooms` is a side table because peer membership, stable room resolution, activity ordering, and open work counts require uniqueness constraints and indexes.
775	- `network_work` is a side table because `work_id` container binding and lifecycle state are safety invariants, not opaque annotations.
776	- `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, and `work_id` are typed columns on timeline/audit rows because routing, filtering, lifecycle validation, and observability depend on them.
777	- JSON metadata is allowed only for opaque provider/runtime annotations that are never used for routing, authorization, filtering, lifecycle continuation, summary counts, or UI primary navigation.
778	- The implementation must not add conversation ownership, room membership, lifecycle state, visibility flags, or work ownership to JSON blobs when a typed column or side table can express the invariant.
779	
780	### RFC 004 Trust Integration
781	
782	RFC 004 currently says v1 uses the v0 wire format and signs the full envelope excluding only `proof.sig`. This change updates RFC 004 so the signed content explicitly includes the new conversation fields.
783	
784	For `agh-network/v1`, the JCS signed envelope includes:
785	
786	- `protocol`
787	- `id`
788	- `kind`
789	- `channel`
790	- `surface` when present
791	- `thread_id` when present
792	- `direct_id` when present
793	- `from`
794	- `to`
795	- `work_id` when present
796	- `reply_to`
797	- `trace_id`
798	- `causation_id`
799	- `ts`
800	- `expires_at`
801	- canonical `body`
802	- canonical `ext`
803	- `proof.profile`, `proof.alg`, `proof.key_id`, and `proof.pubkey`
804	
805	Only `proof.sig` is excluded from canonicalization. Field absence is signed by omission; a receiver must not insert default values before verifying. A verified-format `from` without a valid proof remains `rejected`, not `unverified`.
806	
807	Processing order changes from "route by kind + channel + to" to:
808	
809	1. validate required envelope fields
810	2. reject legacy `interaction_id` and `kind:"direct"`
811	3. evaluate expiration
812	4. evaluate trust state
813	5. route by `channel`, `surface`, matching container ID, and `to`
814	6. apply work lifecycle if `work_id` is present
815	7. apply extension handling
816	
817	NATS request/reply examples must replace `interaction_id` with `work_id`; NATS reply subjects still do not replace core envelope correlation.

1184	  <network-preview encoding="xml-escaped">Inspecting the migration now.</network-preview>
1185	  <network-body encoding="base64-json">BASE64_CANONICAL_JSON</network-body>
1186	</network-message>
1187	```

1189	Bundled `agh-network` skill rewrite outline:
1190	
1191	- Operating model: channel is audience; public threads are N-to-N; direct rooms are restricted 1-to-1.
1192	- Current context: read `AGH_SESSION_ID`, `AGH_SESSION_CHANNEL`, `AGH_PEER_ID`, and wrapper `surface`.
1193	- Inspecting public work: `agh network threads list/show/messages`.
1194	- Inspecting direct rooms: `agh network directs list/resolve/show/messages`.
1195	- Sending in current public thread: `agh network send --surface thread --thread ...`.
1196	- Sending in current direct room: `agh network send --surface direct --direct ...`.
1197	- Work lifecycle: use `--work` only for lifecycle-bearing work; never use it as a conversation ID.
1198	- Handoff: moving public work into a direct room opens a new `work_id`; link with `reply_to`, `trace_id`, and `causation_id`.
1199	- Summarize back: direct-room conclusions are posted publicly as a new `say` in the public thread.
1200	- Prompt injection defense: unchanged, but examples must include `surface`, container ID, and `work_id`.
1201	- Raw token defense: raw `claim_token` remains forbidden.

1246	## Impact Analysis
1247	
1248	| Component | Impact Type | Description and Risk | Required Action |
1249	| --- | --- | --- | --- |
1250	| `docs/rfcs/003_agh-network-v0.md` | modified | normative protocol hard cut | rewrite envelope, kinds, routing, lifecycle, examples |
1251	| `docs/rfcs/004_agh-network-v1.md` | modified | trust RFC references core fields and signed content | update processing model, signed-field set, NATS request/reply examples |
1252	| `docs/_memory/glossary.md` | modified | current kind list includes `direct` | update canonical kind list and add conversation container terms |
1253	| `internal/network/*` | modified | core runtime semantics change | add surface/container validation, deterministic direct ID, work lifecycle rename |
1254	| `internal/store/types.go` | modified | persistence DTOs change | add thread/direct/work/message query DTOs and validation |
1255	| `internal/store/globaldb/*` | modified | persistence/query shape changes | numbered migration, new tables/indexes, same-transaction write path |
1256	| `internal/hooks/*` | modified | new hook family | add async network hook events and payloads |
1257	| `internal/api/contract/*` | modified | public contract break | delete old fields, add new fields, regenerate codegen |
1258	| `internal/api/core/*` | modified | handler/query behavior break | add thread/direct endpoints and send validation |
1259	| `internal/api/udsapi/routes.go` | modified | UDS parity | add same routes as HTTP |
1260	| `internal/cli/*` | modified | operator + agent control plane changes | update send flags, add thread/direct list/detail flows |
1261	| `internal/tools/*` | modified | agent native tools change | update `network_send`, add thread/direct tools |
1262	| `internal/daemon/native_tools.go` | modified | hosted tool dispatch | route new native tool methods through runtime/store |
1263	| `internal/extension/*` | modified | extension manageability | add Host API methods, capability gates, SDK generation |
1264	| `internal/skills/bundled/skills/agh-network/SKILL.md` | modified | agent prompt semantics | rewrite examples and rules |
1265	| `web/src/routes/_app/network*` | modified | route/search model changes | shift from channel/peer rooms to threads/direct rooms |
1266	| `web/src/hooks/routes/use-network-page.ts` | modified | flat view model obsolete | replace with route/query-driven container view models |
1267	| `web/src/systems/network/*` | modified | primary UX changes | thread heads, direct-room list, conversation views |
1268	| `web/e2e/fixtures/*` | modified | artifact schema change | record selected thread/direct instead of selected peer room |
1269	| `packages/site/content/{runtime,protocol}` | modified | docs truth changes | rewrite protocol and operator docs in same PR |

1271	| `web/src/generated/agh-openapi.d.ts` | generated | web type drift risk | regenerate with `make codegen` |
1272	| `.compozy/tasks/_archived/*` | no active change | historical artifacts may keep old terminology | do not edit unless a future archival cleanup explicitly requests it |
1273	
1274	## Safety Invariants
1275	
1276	1. Every conversation-bearing message belongs to exactly one container: `(channel, thread_id)` when `surface="thread"` or `(channel, direct_id)` when `surface="direct"`.
1277	2. `greet` and `whois` never carry `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, or `work_id`.
1278	3. `kind:"direct"` is rejected at every ingress path: network envelope validation, HTTP, UDS, CLI, native tools, extension Host API, tests, docs examples, and generated contract.
1279	4. `interaction_id` is rejected at every public ingress path and removed from active runtime, store, web, docs, and generated artifacts.
1280	5. A `work_id` is durably bound to one conversation container at creation and cannot be continued from another container.
1281	6. Moving work from a public thread to a direct room creates a new `work_id`; linkage uses `reply_to`, `trace_id`, and `causation_id`.
1282	7. Direct-room visibility is restricted to the two room peers plus runtime/audit access; public thread queries never include direct-room messages.
1283	8. Direct-room resolution is deterministic for `(channel, peer_a, peer_b)` regardless of peer order and never creates duplicate active rooms for the same pair.
1284	9. Conversation summaries are derived from committed message rows in the same SQLite transaction that writes the message.
1285	10. Generated API and web types ship in the same change as contract source edits.
1286	11. Agent prompt wrappers always include `channel`, `surface`, the matching conversation ID, `reply_to` when present, and `work_id` only when lifecycle-bearing work exists.
1287	12. Claim-token redaction remains unchanged: raw claim tokens never cross network envelopes, HTTP/UDS responses, CLI output, native tool payloads, logs, prompts, docs examples, web UI, or persisted message bodies.
1288	13. `network_work` never becomes a durable queue; `task_runs` remains the only durable task queue.
1289	14. RFC 004 verified signatures include `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, and `work_id` when present.
1290	15. Direct rooms are exactly two-party in MVP; adding group direct rooms requires a future schema migration and new ADR.
1291	
1292	## Test Strategy
1293	
1294	### Unit Tests
1295	
1296	- Envelope validation for `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, `work_id`, and `kind` hard cuts.
1297	- Legacy rejection tests for `interaction_id` and `kind:"direct"` across network, API contract decoding, CLI input, native tool schema, and Host API.
1298	- Direct-room ID derivation tests proving peer-order independence, channel scoping, same-peer rejection, and stable hash output.
1299	- Work lifecycle tests proving container-scoped `work_id`, terminal rejection, valid state transitions, and cross-container rejection.
1300	- Store DTO validation tests for new summaries, queries, messages, direct rooms, and work entries.
1301	- Wrapper rendering tests proving prompt metadata includes conversation container and `work_id`.
1302	- RFC 004 trust tests proving signed canonical content changes when `surface`, `thread_id`, `direct_id`, or `work_id` changes.
1303	- Hook event catalog tests update expected event count and validate network family matcher fields.
1304	- Native tool schema tests proving `interaction_id` is rejected and raw `claim_token` is still rejected.
1305	
1306	### Migration Tests
1307	
1308	- Fresh DB contains final `network_timeline_log`, `network_threads`, `network_thread_participants`, `network_direct_rooms`, `network_work`, and updated `network_audit_log`.
1309	- Reopen-after-restart preserves the final schema and migration record version 16.
1310	- Old flat `network_timeline_log` rebuild copies only `greet` / `whois` rows and deletes old conversation rows.
1311	- Old `interaction_id` column and old flat timeline indexes are absent after migration.
1312	- Unique `(channel, peer_a, peer_b)` direct-room constraint is present.
1313	- `network_work` rejects invalid surface/container combinations.
1314	- Migration tests run with `-race` where package patterns already use race-sensitive SQLite tests.
1315	
1316	### Integration Tests
1317	
1318	- Router + store public thread creation.
1319	- Router + store direct-room resolve and concurrent resolve under goroutines.
1320	- Work handoff from thread -> direct room opens a new `work_id` and links by `reply_to`, `trace_id`, and `causation_id`.
1321	- Same-transaction write rollback proves no summary update persists if timeline insert fails.
1322	- HTTP/UDS parity tests for every thread/direct/work endpoint.
1323	- CLI integration tests for list/show/messages/resolve/send output shapes.
1324	- Extension Host API tests for read/write capabilities and validation parity.
1325	- Hook dispatch tests proving network hooks fire after durable commit only.
1326	- Web integration tests for channel thread list, thread detail timeline, direct-room list/detail, and composer sending into the active route container.
1327	
1328	### Coverage and Race Requirements
1329	
1330	- Every touched Go package must keep at least 80% package coverage.
1331	- Race-sensitive Go packages touched by this work, especially `internal/network`, `internal/store/globaldb`, `internal/api/core`, `internal/daemon`, and `internal/hooks`, require Linux-Race parity in CI or the closest existing race-enabled lane.
1332	- Web route/system changes require Vitest coverage for route state, query keys, empty/error states, and composer payload shape.
1333	- Contract changes require `make codegen-check` and generated TypeScript drift checks.
1334	
1335	### Required QA Shape
1336	
1337	- `make verify`
1338	- real-scenario QA updates for:
1339	  - public launch thread coordination
1340	  - restricted reviewer handoff via direct room
1341	  - summarize-back-to-thread workflow
1342	  - direct-room resolve race with two agents attempting the same handoff
1343	
1344	## Implementation Steps
1345	
1346	1. RFC and glossary hard cut.
1347	2. Shared runtime types, validators, reason codes, symbol renames, and direct-room ID helper.
1348	3. SQLite migration, store DTOs, store queries, `network_work`, and same-transaction write helper.
1349	4. Router, delivery wrappers, lifecycle handling, hooks, observability, and bundled prompt/skill updates.
1350	5. HTTP/UDS/CLI/native tools/extension Host API contracts and codegen.
1351	6. Web `/network` route tree and systems rewrite.
1352	7. Site docs, extension SDK docs, examples, and MCP/tool docs.
1353	8. Full verification, E2E, real-scenario QA, and task readiness review.
1354	
1355	Technical dependencies:
1356	
1357	- `agh-schema-migration` for numbered SQLite changes.
1358	- `agh-contract-codegen-coship` for OpenAPI + generated TS.
1359	- `cy-web-docs-impact` expectations: web and docs must co-ship with backend contract change.
1360	- `agh-code-guidelines`, `golang-pro`, `nats`, and `agh-test-conventions` for implementation tasks touching Go/network/tests.
1361	
1362	## Monitoring and Observability
1363	
1364	Structured log fields on all network message logs:
1365	
1366	- `channel`
1367	- `surface`
1368	- `thread_id`
1369	- `direct_id`
1370	- `work_id`
1371	- `trace_id`
1372	- `causation_id`
1373	- `kind`
1374	- `peer_from`
1375	- `peer_to`
1376	- `direction`
1377	- `delivery_result`
1378	
1379	Metrics names and cardinality:
1380	
1381	| Metric | Unit | Type | Labels | Cardinality rule |
1382	| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
1383	| `network_messages_total` | messages | counter | `channel`, `surface`, `kind`, `direction`, `result` | no `thread_id`, `direct_id`, `work_id` labels |
1384	| `network_conversation_messages_total` | messages | counter | `channel`, `surface` | no container ID labels |
1385	| `network_threads_open_total` | threads | counter | `channel` | channel only |
1386	| `network_direct_rooms_open_total` | rooms | counter | `channel` | channel only |
1387	| `network_work_open_total` | work items | counter | `channel`, `surface` | no `work_id` label |
1388	| `network_work_transitions_total` | transitions | counter | `channel`, `surface`, `state` | state enum only |
1389	| `network_open_work_items` | work items | gauge | `channel`, `surface` | no `work_id` label |
1390	| `network_delivery_queue_depth` | messages | gauge | `channel`, `surface` | no container ID label |
1391	| `network_direct_resolve_total` | resolves | counter | `channel`, `result` | no peer labels |
1392	
1393	High-cardinality IDs (`thread_id`, `direct_id`, `work_id`, message IDs) belong in structured logs and persisted audit rows, not metric labels.
1394	
1395	Observability path:
1396	
1397	- network runtime updates in-memory status counters for `/network/status`
1398	- durable audit rows include container fields for task/observe aggregations
1399	- network hook events provide extension-visible observation after commit
1400	- no separate event bus is introduced
1401	
1402	## Technical Considerations
1403	
1404	### Key Decisions
1405	
1406	- Public discussion and restricted bilateral discussion are separate conversation containers.
1407	- `work_id` replaces `interaction_id` so lifecycle and conversation no longer share ambiguous language.
1408	- `direct` becomes a conversation surface, not a message kind.
1409	- `say` is the generic conversational event kind on either surface.
1410	- Direct-room resolution is deterministic from channel + sorted peer pair.
1411	- `network_work` is durable lifecycle metadata, not a queue.
1412	- Peer filters remain useful, but they are not the same as direct rooms.
1413	
1414	### Known Risks
1415	
1416	- The change is broad and touches protocol, runtime, docs, CLI, extension APIs, tools, and web in one cut.
1417	- Store transactions need careful idempotency so duplicate message IDs do not inflate summaries.
1418	- RFC 004 canonicalization tests must be updated at the same time as envelope fields or verified-mode peers will drift.
1419	- The current site docs and tests are heavily anchored on `interaction_id` and peer-room wording; partial rewrite will create truth drift immediately.
1420	- Direct rooms are "restricted visibility", not cryptographic privacy; product copy must not overpromise.
1421	
1422	## Architecture Decision Records
1423	
1424	- [ADR-001: Separate Public Threads from Direct Rooms](adrs/adr-001.md) - Public N-to-N conversation and restricted one-to-one conversation become distinct channel-scoped containers.
1425	- [ADR-002: Rename interaction_id to work_id and narrow it to lifecycle-bearing work](adrs/adr-002.md) - Work lifecycle gets a precise name and no longer doubles as a conversation identifier.
1426	- [ADR-003: Make direct a conversation surface, not a message kind](adrs/adr-003.md) - `kind` describes events while `surface` describes where the event lives.
1427
